title: %X format specifier
body:
---


Author Name: **Jonathon Donaldson**
Original Redmine Issue: 926 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2015-06-01
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

Would you be willing to make the %X format specifier display capital hex characters as it does in C/C++? As of right now %x or %X both display all lower-case. I find capital hex letters much easier to read so I always use %X in my C/C++ code. The SV standard doesn't state either way whether %x or %X should result in upper/lower-case characters. Since it's ambiguous in the standard it means that making %X generate upper-case characters would not violate the standard.

So...whaddya say? :)
